surface_forms: the present recession, call it a depression
description: worldwide economic depression (1929–1939)
description_wiki: The Great Depression was a severe global economic downturn from 1929 to 1939.

surface_forms: the shadow of that great foolish tragedy
description: global war originating in Europe, 1914–1918
description_wiki: World War I, or the First World War (28 July 1914 – 11 November 1918), also known as The Great War, was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies (or Entente) and the Central Powers.

description: president of the United States from 1921 to 1923
description_wiki: Warren Gamaliel Harding (November 2, 1865 – August 2, 1923) was the 29th president of the United States, serving from 1921 until his death in 1923.

description: president of the United States from 1923 to 1929
description_wiki: Calvin Coolidge (born John Calvin Coolidge Jr.;  KOOL-ij; July 4, 1872 – January 5, 1933) was the 30th president of the United States, serving from 1923 to 1929.

description: president of the United States from 1913 to 1921 (1856–1924)
description_wiki: Thomas Woodrow Wilson (December 28, 1856 – February 3, 1924) was the 28th president of the United States, serving from 1913 to 1921.

description: 20th-century international organisation, predecessor to the United Nations
description_wiki: The League of Nations (LN or LoN; French: Société des Nations [sɔsjete de nɑsjɔ̃], SdN) was the first worldwide intergovernmental organisation whose principal mission was to maintain world peace.

surface_forms: Harnett County, when I was a child
description: county in North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Harnett County ( HAR-nit) is a county located in the U.S. state of North Carolina.

description: 1861–1865 civil war in the United States between the North and the South over the enslavement of African-Americans
description_wiki: The American Civil War (April 12, 1861 – May 26, 1865; also known by other names) was a civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened because of the election of Abraham Lincoln and the growing abolitionist movement in the North.

description: American writer (1902–1968)
description_wiki: John Ernst Steinbeck ( STYNE-bek; February 27, 1902 – December 20, 1968) was an American writer and novelist.

description: 1939 novel by John Steinbeck
description_wiki: The Grapes of Wrath is an American realist novel written by John Steinbeck and published in 1939.

description: 1939–1945 global conflict
description_wiki: World War II, or the Second World War (1 September 1939 – 2 September 1945), was a global conflict between two coalitions: the Allies and the Axis powers.

description: central banking system of the United States
economic programs of U.S. president Franklin D. Roosevelt
description_wiki: The New Deal was a 1933–1938 series of economic, social, and political reforms in response to the Great Depression in the United States under President Franklin D. Roosevelt.

description: town in Dare County, North Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Manteo ( MAN-ee-oh) is a town in Dare County, North Carolina, United States, located on Roanoke Island.

description: United States federal government public work relief program part of the New Deal
description_wiki: The Civilian Conservation Corps (CCC) was a government work relief program that ran from 1933 to 1942 in the United States for unemployed, unmarried men ages 18–25 and eventually expanded to ages 17–28, who volunteered amid widespread unemployment.

description: United States government New Deal agency (1935–1939) employing millions of unemployed people to carry out public works projects
description_wiki: The Works Progress Administration (WPA; from 1935 to 1939, then known as the Work Projects Administration from 1939 to 1943) was an American New Deal agency that employed millions of jobseekers (mostly men who were not formally educated) to carry out public works projects, including the construction of public buildings and roads.

description: American multinational oil and gas corporation
description_wiki: Exxon Mobil Corporation ( EK-son MOH-bəl) is an American multinational oil and gas corporation headquartered in Spring, Texas, a suburb of Houston.

description: American academic administrator (1822–1882)
description_wiki: Braxton Craven (August 22, 1822 – November 7, 1882) was an American educator.

description: country in Eastern Europe and Northern Asia
description_wiki: Russia, or the Russian Federation, is a country in Eastern Europe and North Asia.

description: federal city and former capital of Russia
description_wiki: Saint Petersburg, formerly known as Petrograd (Петроград) and later Leningrad (Ленинград), is the second-largest city in Russia, after Moscow, the nation's capital.

description: capital and most populous city of Russia
description_wiki: Moscow is the capital and largest city of Russia, standing on the Moskva River in Central Russia.

description: capital city of the United States of America
description_wiki: Washington, D.C., officially the District of Columbia and commonly known as simply Washington or D.C., is the capital city and federal district of the United States.

aliases: The City So Nice They Named It Twice
description: most populous city in the United States
description_wiki: New York, often called New York City (NYC), is the most populous city in the United States.

description: president of the United States from 1933 to 1945 (1882–1945)
description_wiki: Franklin Delano Roosevelt (January 30, 1882 – April 12, 1945), also known as FDR, was the 32nd president of the United States, serving from 1933 until his death in 1945.
